timeline.event: Pilot Bank A compliance team begins noticing empty reports (customer-reported, not yet confirmed by platform metrics)
agent-runtime v2.3.1 deployed with async task completion reporting optimization
completed_without_artifact rises from 0.2/min to 1.1/min; write latency P99 spikes to 890ms
T-2001 filed: agent task reports completed before writing artifact
On-call engineer Alex picks up alert, initially suspects storage backend
Pattern identified: status-ordering bug, write confirmation never fires
T-2002 filed: batch of 12 tasks, only 4 have output artifacts
Peak anomaly: 33 tasks/min completing, 6.2/min without artifacts
T-2003 (critical): Pilot Bank A escalation, 8 empty reports in audit pipeline
Correlation with v2.3.1 deployment confirmed by engineering
Formal escalation email from Sarah Chen, VP Compliance, Pilot Bank A
v2.3.2 fix ready, restores write-ack-first-then-complete flow
v2.3.2 hotfix deployed to all 8 nodes
All metrics return to baseline; write latency P99 back to 340ms
OK alerts: all metrics returned to normal
Post-hotfix verification: all nodes healthy, sample runs confirmed

confirmed_facts: v2.3.1 was deployed at 09:15 UTC on 2025-06-12 (deployment-log.md, team-notes.md)
The deployment changed status transition ordering to report completed before write confirmation (deployment-log.md, team-notes.md)
completed_without_artifact spiked from 0.2/min to 6.2/min in the 09:15-10:30 window (system-metrics.txt, team-notes.md)
Write confirmation latency P99 spiked from 312ms to 2100ms (system-metrics.txt, team-notes.md)
Pilot Bank A was the escalated customer with 8 empty reports (customer-email.txt, tickets.json T-2003)
v2.3.2 hotfix resolved the issue with all metrics returning to baseline by 11:45 (system-metrics.txt, deployment-log.md)
The DASHBOARD UI V2 REDESIGN section in team-notes.md is explicitly a separate, unrelated project
likely_root_cause: agent-runtime v2.3.1 changed the task completion pipeline to report 'completed' status before verifying the async artifact write succeeded, causing tasks to appear complete with no output.
supporting_evidence: deployment-log.md: v2.3.1 change log — 'Optimized async task completion reporting pipeline' and 'Updated status transition logic'
team-notes.md: 'the new optimization reports complete first, then writes asynchronously — but there's no check that the async write actually succeeded'
system-metrics.txt: completed_without_artifact spikes immediately after 09:15 deployment and returns to baseline after 11:30 hotfix
deployment-log.md: v2.3.2 change log — 'Rolled back async completion reporting changes from v2.3.1'
inferences: Customer claim of issues starting at 08:00 is likely imprecise; the metrics inflection point is clearly at 09:15
Write confirmation latency spike (to 2100ms P99) suggests async write queue backup as tasks completed without verification
At least some of the 8 Pilot Bank A empty reports were accepted into downstream audit pipeline before detection
Pilot Bank A's 08:00 timeline may reflect pre-existing low-rate issues or imprecise reporting
unknowns: Exact total number of affected tasks across all customers (rates known, cumulative counts not)
Whether any customer data was exposed or misrouted (not answerable from available files)
Whether SLA breach threshold was crossed (requires contractual definition and cumulative counts)
Root cause of underlying write failures — storage backend reported healthy, so what caused writes to fail?
Per-customer breakdown of affected task counts
recommended_actions: Implement post-deployment metric gate on completed_without_artifact with automatic 5-minute rollback threshold
Add task completion contract tests to CI: verify artifact existence, non-emptiness, and checksum before reporting completed
Conduct post-incident review with Pilot Bank A to confirm audit pipeline impact and provide preventive measures assurance
information_gaps: Agent-runtime service logs for the 09:15-11:30 window (would confirm exact task IDs affected)
Storage backend (S3) access logs for the same window (would confirm write success/failure patterns)
Per-customer breakdown of affected tasks (would quantify impact beyond Pilot Bank A)
The specific code diff between v2.3.0 and v2.3.1 (would confirm exact status transition change)
Cumulative count of affected tasks (metrics only show per-minute rates)
